examples.title: Algorithm: Process Scheduling with Ready Queue
examples.description: Demonstrates how the OS manages processes using scheduling queues and state transitions
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ProcessScheduler
INPUT: List of processes with arrival time and burst time
OUTPUT: Order of process execution and completion times

// Define Process Control Block structure
DATASTRUCTURE PCB
 PROCESS_ID
 ARRIVAL_TIME
 BURST_TIME
 REMAINING_TIME
 PRIORITY
 STATE // NEW, READY, RUNNING, WAITING, TERMINATED
 WAITING_TIME
 TURNAROUND_TIME
ENDDATASTRUCTURE

// Initialize scheduling queues
CREATE EMPTY_LIST ready_queue
CREATE EMPTY_LIST completed_processes
CREATE current_time = 0
CREATE running_process = NULL

// Function to add new processes to ready queue
PROCEDURE add_to_ready_queue(process)
 process.STATE = READY
 APPEND process TO ready_queue
 SORT ready_queue BY priority (or arrival time)
ENDPROCEDURE

// Function to perform context switch
PROCEDURE context_switch(from_process, to_process)
 IF from_process IS NOT NULL THEN
 from_process.STATE = READY
 from_process.CPU_REGISTERS = save_current_state()
 APPEND from_process TO ready_queue
 ENDIF
 
 to_process = REMOVE_FIRST from ready_queue
 to_process.STATE = RUNNING
 restore_process_state(to_process)
 RETURN to_process
ENDPROCEDURE

// Main scheduling loop (Short-term scheduler)
PROCEDURE schedule_processes(process_list)
 // Add all processes that have arrived
 FOR EACH process IN process_list DO
 IF process.ARRIVAL_TIME <= current_time THEN
 add_to_ready_queue(process)
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 
 WHILE ready_queue IS NOT EMPTY OR running_process IS NOT NULL DO
 // Dispatch process to CPU
 IF running_process IS NULL AND ready_queue IS NOT EMPTY THEN
 running_process = context_switch(NULL, ready_queue)
 ENDIF
 
 // Execute process for time quantum
 IF running_process IS NOT NULL THEN
 time_slice = MIN(QUANTUM, running_process.REMAINING_TIME)
 current_time = current_time + time_slice
 running_process.REMAINING_TIME = running_process.REMAINING_TIME - time_slice
 
 // Check for process completion
 IF running_process.REMAINING_TIME == 0 THEN
 running_process.STATE = TERMINATED
 running_process.TURNAROUND_TIME = current_time - running_process.ARRIVAL_TIME
 APPEND running_process TO completed_processes
 running_process = NULL
 ENDIF
 ENDIF
 
 // Add newly arrived processes to ready queue
 FOR EACH process IN process_list DO
 IF process.ARRIVAL_TIME <= current_time AND process.STATE == NEW THEN
 add_to_ready_queue(process)
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 ENDWHILE
 
 RETURN completed_processes
ENDPROCEDURE

// Calculate average waiting time and turnaround time
PROCEDURE calculate_metrics(completed_processes)
 total_waiting = 0
 total_turnaround = 0
 FOR EACH process IN completed_processes DO
 process.WAITING_TIME = process.TURNAROUND_TIME - process.BURST_TIME
 total_waiting = total_waiting + process.WAITING_TIME
 total_turnaround = total_turnaround + process.TURNAROUND_TIME
 ENDFOR
 PRINT "Average Waiting Time: " + (total_waiting / COUNT(completed_processes))
 PRINT "Average Turnaround Time: " + (total_turnaround / COUNT(completed_processes))
ENDPROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the core concepts of process scheduling: 1) PCB as a data structure holding all process information, 2) Ready queue management for processes waiting to execute, 3) Context switch mechanism saving/restoring process state, 4) State transitions between NEW, READY, RUNNING, and TERMINATED states, 5) The scheduling loop that dispatches processes to CPU

examples.title: Python: Round Robin Process Scheduling Simulation
examples.description: Implements Round Robin CPU scheduling algorithm with context switching and process state management
examples.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Round Robin Process Scheduling Algorithm
==========================================
This simulation demonstrates how an OS schedules processes using the Round Robin
algorithm, managing the ready queue and performing context switches.
"""

from collections import deque
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum
import time

class ProcessState(Enum):
 NEW = "New"
 READY = "Ready"
 RUNNING = "Running"
 WAITING = "Waiting"
 TERMINATED = "Terminated"

@dataclass
class PCB:
 """Process Control Block - stores all information about a process"""
 pid: int
 arrival_time: int
 burst_time: int
 remaining_time: int
 priority: int
 state: ProcessState
 waiting_time: int = 0
 turnaround_time: int = 0
 completion_time: int = 0
 
 def __init__(self, pid, arrival, burst, priority=0):
 self.pid = pid
 self.arrival_time = arrival
 self.burst_time = burst
 self.remaining_time = burst
 self.priority = priority
 self.state = ProcessState.NEW
 self.waiting_time = 0
 self.turnaround_time = 0
 self.completion_time = 0

class RoundRobinScheduler:
 """Round Robin CPU Scheduler implementation"""
 
 def __init__(self, time_quantum=4):
 self.time_quantum = time_quantum
 self.ready_queue = deque() # Queue for ready processes
 self.waiting_queue = deque() # Queue for I/O waiting processes
 self.completed = []
 self.current_time = 0
 self.current_process = None
 self.context_switches = 0
 
 def add_process(self, process):
 """Add a new process to the ready queue"""
 process.state = ProcessState.READY
 self.ready_queue.append(process)
 
 def context_switch(self, incoming_process):
 """Perform context switch between processes"""
 self.context_switches += 1
 
 if self.current_process and self.current_process.remaining_time > 0:
 # Save current process state back to ready queue
 self.current_process.state = ProcessState.READY
 self.ready_queue.append(self.current_process)
 
 # Load new process
 if incoming_process:
 incoming_process.state = ProcessState.RUNNING
 self.current_process = incoming_process
 else:
 self.current_process = None
 
 def simulate_io_wait(self, process, io_duration):
 """Simulate a process waiting for I/O"""
 process.state = ProcessState.WAITING
 self.current_process = None
 # In a real OS, this would go to device queue
 # For simulation, we'll track it separately
 process.remaining_io = io_duration
 self.waiting_queue.append(process)
 
 def check_io_completion(self):
 """Check if any I/O operations are complete"""
 temp_queue = deque()
 while self.waiting_queue:
 process = self.waiting_queue.popleft()
 process.remaining_io -= 1
 if process.remaining_io <= 0:
 process.state = ProcessState.READY
 self.ready_queue.append(process)
 else:
 temp_queue.append(process)
 self.waiting_queue = temp_queue
 
 def execute(self, processes):
 """Execute the scheduling simulation"""
 # Sort processes by arrival time
 processes.sort(key=lambda p: p.arrival_time)
 
 arrival_index = 0
 n = len(processes)
 
 print("=" * 60)
 print("ROUND ROBIN SCHEDULING SIMULATION")
 print(f"Time Quantum: {self.time_quantum}")
 print("=" * 60)
 
 # Add initially arrived processes
 self._add_arrived_processes(processes, arrival_index, 0)
 
 while self.ready_queue or self.waiting_queue or self.current_process:
 # If no process running, dispatch one
 if not self.current_process and self.ready_queue:
 next_process = self.ready_queue.popleft()
 self.context_switch(next_process)
 print(f"Time {self.current_time}: Context Switch - Process {next_process.pid} now RUNNING")
 
 # Execute current process for time quantum or until completion
 if self.current_process:
 execution_time = min(self.time_quantum, self.current_process.remaining_time)
 
 for _ in range(execution_time):
 self.current_time += 1
 self.current_process.remaining_time -= 1
 
 # Check for newly arrived processes
 self._add_arrived_processes(processes, arrival_index, self.current_time)
 
 # Check I/O completion
 self.check_io_completion()
 
 print(f" -> Process {self.current_process.pid} executed for {execution_time} units")
 print(f" Remaining time: {self.current_process.remaining_time}")
 
 # Check if process completed
 if self.current_process.remaining_time == 0:
 self.current_process.completion_time = self.current_time
 self.current_process.turnaround_time = \
 self.current_process.completion_time - self.current_process.arrival_time
 self.current_process.waiting_time = \
 self.current_process.turnaround_time - self.current_process.burst_time
 self.current_process.state = ProcessState.TERMINATED
 self.completed.append(self.current_process)
 print(f" -> Process {self.current_process.pid} COMPLETED!")
 self.current_process = None
 else:
 # Put back to ready queue (time quantum exhausted)
 self.context_switch(None)
 else:
 # CPU idle - advance time
 if self.ready_queue:
 continue
 self.current_time += 1
 self._add_arrived_processes(processes, arrival_index, self.current_time)
 self.check_io_completion()
 
 return self._calculate_metrics()
 
 def _add_arrived_processes(self, processes, arrival_index, current_time):
 """Add processes that have arrived by current_time"""
 while arrival_index < len(processes):
 if processes[arrival_index].arrival_time <= current_time:
 if processes[arrival_index].state == ProcessState.NEW:
 self.add_process(processes[arrival_index])
 print(f"Time {current_time}: Process {processes[arrival_index].pid} entered READY queue")
 arrival_index += 1
 else:
 break
 return arrival_index
 
 def _calculate_metrics(self):
 """Calculate and return scheduling metrics"""
 total_waiting = sum(p.waiting_time for p in self.completed)
 total_turnaround = sum(p.turnaround_time for p in self.completed)
 n = len(self.completed)
 
 avg_waiting = total_waiting / n if n > 0 else 0
 avg_turnaround = total_turnaround / n if n > 0 else 0
 
 print("\n" + "=" * 60)
 print("SCHEDULING RESULTS")
 print("=" * 60)
 print(f"{'PID':<6}{'AT':<6}{'BT':<6}{'CT':<6}{'TAT':<6}{'WT':<6}")
 print("-" * 36)
 for p in sorted(self.completed, key=lambda x: x.pid):
 print(f"{p.pid:<6}{p.arrival_time:<6}{p.burst_time:<6}{p.completion_time:<6}{p.turnaround_time:<6}{p.waiting_time:<6}")
 print("-" * 36)
 print(f"\nAverage Waiting Time: {avg_waiting:.2f}")
 print(f"Average Turnaround Time: {avg_turnaround:.2f}")
 print(f"Total Context Switches: {self.context_switches}")
 
 return {
 'avg_waiting_time': avg_waiting,
 'avg_turnaround_time': avg_turnaround,
 'context_switches': self.context_switches
 }

# Main execution
if __name__ == "__main__":
 # Create processes: (PID, Arrival Time, Burst Time, Priority)
 processes = [
 PCB(1, 0, 10, 2),
 PCB(2, 1, 5, 1),
 PCB(3, 2, 8, 3),
 PCB(4, 3, 6, 1),
 ]
 
 # Run Round Robin scheduler with time quantum = 4
 scheduler = RoundRobinScheduler(time_quantum=4)
 scheduler.execute(processes)
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates: 1) PCB as a dataclass storing process information, 2) Ready queue management using deque, 3) Round Robin scheduling with time quantum, 4) Context switching between processes, 5) State transitions (NEW→READY→RUNNING→TERMINATED), 6) Calculation of waiting time and turnaround time metrics
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × q) where n is number of processes and q is time quantum
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing processes and queues

examples.title: C: Process Control Block and FCFS Scheduling
examples.description: Implements PCB structure and First-Come-First-Served scheduling algorithm in C
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates: 1) PCB structure with all essential fields (PID, state, timing info), 2) Process queue implementation using linked list, 3) FCFS scheduling algorithm, 4) Context switch concept showing how process state is saved/restored, 5) State transitions and timing calculations. The code shows how the OS manages the ready queue and dispatches processes to CPU.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n²) for FCFS due to nested loop structure in worst case
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing process control blocks

examples.title: Java: Multiple Scheduling Algorithms Implementation
examples.description: Implements FCFS, SJF, and Priority scheduling algorithms with process management
examples.explanation: This Java implementation demonstrates: 1) PCB as a class with all process attributes, 2) Four major CPU scheduling algorithms: FCFS, SJF, Priority, and Round Robin, 3) Ready queue management, 4) Context switching tracking, 5) State transitions, 6) Timing metrics calculation (waiting time, turnaround time). The code shows how different scheduling policies affect process execution order and average waiting times.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n²) for FCFS/SJF/Priority; O(n×q) for Round Robin where q is time quantum
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing process control blocks and queues
